reason: Both residual findings are minor hints_missing issues. The section content already contains the missing terms (AuthenticationFailedException appears in the stack trace example in s3 body; UPDATED_USER_ID appears in the table and code example in s4 body), so content-based retrieval is unaffected. The hint gaps create minor inconsistencies but do not block agent access to the knowledge. Neither finding reaches medium impact.

findings_assessment.justification: AuthenticationFailedException is only used as an illustrative stack trace example in s3, not as a primary concept. The existing hint PasswordAuthenticator already identifies the same example context — a user searching for AuthenticationFailedException would typically also know about PasswordAuthenticator. The fix agent did not apply the change (Phase E output shows identical hints to Phase D), but the omission has low retrieval impact because the term also appears verbatim in the s3 section body.

UPDATED_USER_ID is the third of three parallel column names; the other two (INSERT_REQUEST_ID, INSERT_EXECUTION_ID) are already in hints. The term also appears explicitly in the s4 section content (markdown table and log output example: UPDATED_USER_ID=batch_user), so content-based retrieval is not blocked. The fix agent did not apply the change (Phase E output shows identical hints to Phase D), creating a minor inconsistency but no meaningful retrieval gap.
